functions.description: Executes a command. Some commands can take a list of objects as parameters. See \ref globalcommandsids for the available commands.

command.description: The ID of the command to execute. See \ref globalcommandsids for the lists of commands.

objects.description: An array of objects. Each object is an ID (GUID), name, or path of the object. Some commands can take objects as arguments. See the commands for more information.

items.description: A path to a file. Some commands can take files as arguments. See the commands for more information.

files.description: An array of files. Each object is an path to a file. Some commands can take files as arguments. See the commands for more information.

platforms.description: An array of platform. Each platform is an ID (GUID) or a unique name. Some commands can take platforms as arguments. See the commands for more information.

value.description: A value to pass to the command. Some commands can take a value as an argument. See the commands for more information.

examples.title: Finding and selecting objects in Project Explorer
examples.description: Finds the specified objects in the Project Explorer view (Selection Channel 1), and selects them.

examples.title: Executing a command requiring the object's parameter
examples.description: Executes "ConvertCurrentPlatform" on objects defined by "objects" GUID's.

examples.title: Executing a command not requiring the object's parameter
Muting the specified object
examples.description: Executes the "Mute" command and specify the mute state.

examples.title: Toggling the Mute state on the specified object
examples.description: Executes the "Mute" command without specifying a value to toggle the current state.
